Add Verb Conjugation page (/conjugation)

- New page with 4 conjugation groups based on 3rd person ending:
  Group 1 (-a/-o): dirbti, gyventi, skambinti, bėgti, rasti, miegoti
  Group 2 (-o/-ė): rašyti, skaityti, valgyti, mokyti, žinoti, matyti

  Group 4 (-i/-ėjo): norėti, mylėti, turėti, galėti, tikėti
- Special/irregular verbs section: būti, eiti, kalbėti, galvoti
- Each group shows: endings table + primary verb full conjugation side by side

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Pass WEATHER_API_KEY to both Python and Blazor apps in deployment

The WEATHER_API_KEY GitHub secret was not being passed to the Python
container app via the workflow (it was only set manually in Azure Portal).
The Blazor app was referencing it correctly as WeatherApiKey but the
secret did not exist in GitHub, so it deployed with an empty value,
causing 'WEATHER_API_KEY is not configured' warnings and no weather data.

- Add WEATHER_API_KEY to the Python app's --set-env-vars so it is
  consistently managed via GitHub secrets alongside all other config
- Add a warning in the validation step if WEATHER_API_KEY is not set
  so the misconfiguration is visible in the workflow log

Action required: add WEATHER_API_KEY to GitHub Actions secrets at
Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add UseStaticFiles to fix _framework/blazor.web.js 404 in Azure

MapStaticAssets() is endpoint-based: it registers routes in endpoint
routing and requires the staticwebassets.endpoints.json manifest to be
resolved at startup.  In production Docker (ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=
Production), the endpoint for the non-fingerprinted route
_framework/blazor.web.js was not being matched — resulting in a 404.
The browser could not load Blazor's JavaScript, so the circuit never
started and all interactive components (search box, theme toggle) were
inert prerendered elements with no behaviour.

CSS links work because @Assets[] returns fingerprinted URLs
(app.abc123.css) which ARE matched by MapStaticAssets().  The Blazor
script tag emits the non-fingerprinted _framework/blazor.web.js URL,
which was the one falling through to a 404.

Fix: add app.UseStaticFiles() as middleware before endpoint routing.
UseStaticFiles serves any file found in wwwroot/ directly, bypassing
endpoint routing entirely.  It handles _framework/blazor.web.js (and
all other wwwroot/ files) as a reliable fallback.  MapStaticAssets()
is kept for its fingerprinted-URL long-cache-header behaviour.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix InsightsView filter selection and empty detail pane

- Add onChange handlers for selectedType and showDismissed so
  autoSelectFirst() fires whenever the active filter changes, not
  just when the filtered count happens to differ. This ensures the
  list selection (and detail pane) always tracks the current filter.

- In InsightDetailView, replace "No detail provided." with the
  insight title when detail is empty, so the detail section always
  shows useful content.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: disable JWT session management on service-role client to prevent Invalid Compact JWS

- getServiceClient() now passes auth: { persistSession: false, autoRefreshToken: false, detectSessionInUrl: false }
  so the service client never tries to parse session cookies as JWTs
- upload-image route: guard against missing SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
- Updated NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY on Vercel to current sb_publishable_ format

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Blazor Server interactive components not loading in Azure

Azure Container Apps terminates TLS at the ingress and forwards
HTTP internally. Without UseForwardedHeaders(), the app sees the
internal HTTP scheme instead of HTTPS. This caused two problems:

1. Blazor Server's antiforgery middleware rejected the SignalR
   circuit negotiate request because the Origin header from the
   browser (https://…) didn't match the server-perceived scheme
   (http://…). The circuit never connected, so interactive
   components (FluentSearch, FluentSwitch) stayed as inert
   prerendered custom elements that the browser couldn't activate.

2. UseHsts() silently did nothing because the app thought it was
   serving HTTP, so the Strict-Transport-Security header was never
   sent.

Fixes:
- Add ForwardedHeadersOptions trusting X-Forwarded-For and
  X-Forwarded-Proto from any proxy (Azure CA's proxy IPs are
  not fixed, so KnownNetworks/KnownProxies are cleared).
- Call app.UseForwardedHeaders() as the very first middleware so
  all downstream middleware (antiforgery, HSTS, HTTPS redirect)
  see the correct scheme and client IP.

Also add sticky session affinity to the deployment workflow.
Blazor Server's SignalR circuit must stay on the same replica
for the browser session lifetime; without it, requests can hit
a replica that has no record of the circuit, silently breaking
all interactive components.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(auth): replace client-side signOut with server-side /api/auth/signout route

Client supabase.auth.signOut() hangs on slow connections, blocking the
window.location.href redirect so users stay stuck on /account.

New approach:
- GET /api/auth/signout — server Route Handler that calls signOut() with
  full cookie access and redirects to origin /
- AccountSignOut becomes a plain <a href> — no JS, no async, no hang
- Test updated: checks link role instead of button, verifies session cleared

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(build): remove duplicate middleware.ts (conflicts with proxy.ts in Next 16)

Next.js 16 uses proxy.ts instead of middleware.ts. proxy.ts already handles
Supabase session refresh + /admin and /account route protection, so
middleware.ts was redundant and caused a fatal build error.

Replace the inline SVG with the actual Poké Ball photo in BrandIcon.

fix(nav): catch getBrowserClient crash when Supabase env vars missing

If NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL/ANON_KEY are not set on the host, createBrowserClient
throws synchronously, crashing NavAuthLinks entirely (login link disappears).
Now catches the error and gracefully falls back to showing the LOGIN link.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(auth): show password-updated banner on account page; surface callback errors on login page

- Account page: read ?updated=true query param, show green success banner

  actionable message so expired confirmation links don't silently fail

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(auth): add middleware session refresh, fix nav LOGIN visibility, improve sign-up UX

- Add src/middleware.ts: Supabase SSR session refresh on every request (prevents
  silent logout when access token expires; required for SSR auth to work)
- NavAuthLinks: show LOGIN link immediately during hydration (opacity 0.5) instead
  of rendering null — fixes invisible nav link on live site
- Login page: replace alert() on sign-up success with inline confirmation UI;
  add signupSent state with 'Go to Sign In' button

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: flatten @Nested tests to avoid empty Surefire XML files

JUnit 5 @Nested classes cause Maven Surefire to emit an empty outer
XML file (tests=0) alongside the nested-class XML files. The Testream
junit-reporter's junit-to-ctrf converter crashes when it encounters a
<testsuite tests="0"> element with no <testcase> children.

Refactor all three test classes to use flat methods instead of @Nested.
Each @DisplayName now prefixes the group name (e.g. 'formatPrice - ...'),
preserving readability while generating a single, well-formed XML file
per class that the reporter can process without errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Power Query stability diagnostics, CLI backward-compat fix, and crash isolation tests (#560)

* Log: McCauley, Nate, Hanna stability investigation planning

Stability Bug Investigation (2026-03-21T06-14-44Z)
- McCauley reviewed last 10 PRs, produced investigation sequence
- Nate mapped 45+ existing tests, identified serial workflow gaps
- Hanna reviewed COM patterns, validated cleanup correctness

Findings:
- Existing tests prove command-level correctness; gaps in serial workflows
- Serial Power Query create → refresh → modify → reopen sequence not tested
- Post-timeout recovery and workbook reopen paths not explicitly validated
- COM cleanup patterns are sound; no code changes needed

Recommendation: regression-test-first approach (baseline → edge → recovery)

Updated:
- Orchestration logs for McCauley, Nate, Hanna (3 files)
- Session log: stability investigation plan summary
- Agent history files with team updates

* Add serial workflow regression tests for timeout/recovery

Two new test suites validate multi-operation timeout recovery patterns:

ExcelBatchSerialWorkflowTests (3 tests):
- SerialWorkflow_TimeoutInMiddle_LaterOperationsFailFast
- SerialWorkflow_ReopenAfterTimeout_NewSessionWorks
- SerialWorkflow_MultipleTimeouts_AllFollowUpOperationsFailFast

SessionManagerSerialWorkflowTests (4 tests):
- SerialWorkflow_GetSessionAfterTimeout_ReturnsNullOrFailsFast
- SerialWorkflow_CreateSessionAfterTimeoutCleanup_SucceedsOnSameFile
- SerialWorkflow_ActiveSessionCount_RemainsAccurateThroughTimeout
- SerialWorkflow_MultipleSessionsOneTimeout_OthersUnaffected

All tests PASS, confirming current implementation handles serial
timeout recovery correctly. These serve as regression baselines for
the workflow patterns described in the v1.8.32 bug report.

Test patterns modeled:
- Operation A (success) -> Operation B (timeout) -> Operation C (attempt)
- Timeout + dispose -> immediate reopen same file
- Multiple sessions, one timeout -> others unaffected
- GetSession/CloseSession/CreateSession behavior after timeout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* .squad: Log pivot decision — escalate defect search above ComInterop layer

Serial workflow regression tests (7 tests) all GREEN. Current control layer
(ComInterop/session/timeout) is healthy. Any remaining bug lives above this
layer in CLI daemon state, service routing, or workbook-specific patterns.

- Cheritto: Trace wrapper seam for where bug can survive

validate existing correct behavior as regression baselines. Focus shifts from
timeout recovery to layer-above investigation.

All control tests PASSED; no RED regression found at current visibility.

  * Nate's test results: 7 ComInterop control tests all PASSED

commit.message:
fix: handle IFDRational and other EXIF types in sidecar JSON

piexif IFDRational values from JPEG EXIF data are not JSON-serializable.
Add a _MetaEncoder fallback that converts Rational-like objects (anything
with numerator/denominator) to float, preventing pre-decode failures on
images with rich EXIF metadata.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: imganalyzer
repository.description: CLI tool to analyze images and camera RAW files — outputs Lightroom-compatible XMP sidecars with EXIF metadata, technical quality scores, and AI-powered content analysis

commit.message:
fix: trigger decode replenishment from status poll

_replenish_decode_buffer() was only called from jobs/claim (worker
requests) and image ingest.  When no distributed workers are actively
claiming, the pre-decoder stops receiving new work — even though the
machine is idle.

Now also triggered from _handle_status() which runs every ~1s from
dashboard polling, ensuring continuous decode progress.  The internal
5s throttle prevents excessive DB queries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

tables, and blockquote callouts left unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

tune: reduce decode feed batch to 10 images max, min buffer 5

Smaller batches give the resource sampler finer-grained control over
disk and CPU pressure.  The replenish loop runs every 5s, so at 10
images per feed the pipeline still sustains ~2 images/s throughput
while avoiding large bursts of disk I/O.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: adaptive resource-aware pre-decode scheduling

Replace fixed buffer threshold (in_flight < 100) with CPU and disk I/O
monitoring via psutil.  The pre-decoder now:

- Samples CPU% and disk busy% every 5s via ResourceSampler
- Feeds large batches when system is idle (CPU < 40%, disk < 50%)
- Reduces batch size under moderate load
- Stops feeding when CPU > 70% AND disk > 80% (except to maintain
  a minimum buffer of 20 to prevent worker starvation)
- Scales default decode threads from 2 to min(cpu_count - 2, 6),
  since RAW demosaic releases the GIL and parallelises effectively
- Exposes resource metrics (cpu_pct, disk_read_mbps, disk_busy_pct)
  in the status response for dashboard visibility

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: iris
repository.description: AMD RAD's multi-GPU Triton-based framework for seamless multi-GPU programming

commit.message:
fix: decode buffer stalls when cached-pending count exceeds threshold

The buffer check counted cached images with ANY pending module. With
4,661 cached images × ~6 modules each, cached_pending was always >> 100
so the buffer appeared 'full' and decoding stopped permanently.

Fix: measure decode queue depth (in-flight = queued - done - failed)
instead of cached-pending-image count. This tracks how much work the
pre-decoder has left, not how many cached images exist. The decoder
continuously feeds new batches as in-flight work drops below the
buffer size.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: hybrid per-user question dedup for board modes

Replace the global used=true-forever approach with a two-layer strategy:

Layer 1 — used=true as a game-lifetime lock:
- Questions are returned to the pool (used=false) when a game finishes
  or is abandoned, not left permanently consumed.
- All pool_question_ids are now returned on game end, not just unanswered
  ones. user_question_history provides the per-user protection going forward.

Layer 2 — user_question_history for 60-day per-user dedup:
- draw_board() now accepts p_user_ids uuid[] and excludes any question
  already in any participant's history. Drawn questions are atomically
  inserted into user_question_history for all participants.
- When a guest joins an existing online/duel game the host already drew,
  the board's question IDs are recorded in the guest's history via
  record_board_question_history() — no redraw needed.
- Duel createGame/joinQueue pre-fetches host's seen IDs and passes them
  as excludeIds to drawForDuel() to avoid host duplicates.
- cleanup_old_question_history() window extended to 60 days (was 30).
  Daily cleanup cron keeps the table bounded, eliminating the need for
  seen_at filters in queries (pure PK point-lookups, sub-millisecond even
  at 100M rows).

New DB functions (migration 20260414000000):
- draw_board(p_exclude_ids, p_user_ids) — extended signature
- record_board_question_history(user_ids, question_ids) — guest join
- reset_board_history_if_exhausted(user_id) — auto-reset at 80% pool seen
- cleanup_old_question_history() — updated to 60-day window

Backend:
- question-pool.service.ts: drawBoard(excludeNews, allowFallback, userIds)
  + recordBoardHistory() helper
- supabase.service.ts: getSeenQuestionIds() removes inline time filter
- game.types.ts: CreateGameDto gets optional playerIds[]
- game.service.ts: passes playerIds to drawBoard; finishSession returns all
- online-game.service.ts: passes hostId on draw; records guest history on
  join; returns all pool questions on finish/abandon
- duel.service.ts: excludes host seen history on draw; records guest history
  on join; returns all pool questions on abandon

Pool sizing impact: a pool of 5k-10k questions comfortably supports
thousands of active users with no repeats within 60 days.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

style: add padding to stat blocks, move progress/stats above tasks

Reordered: Agents → Messages → Inbox Log → Progress/Stats → Tasks.
Task-related elements are now grouped together.

commit.message:
feat: demand-driven pre-decode scheduling with buffer replenishment

Replace the one-shot 'decode everything' approach with an incremental
demand-driven system that keeps a configurable buffer of decoded images
ahead of workers:

PreDecoder:
- Add feed() method for incremental batch submission (reuses executor)
- Track _queued_ids to avoid duplicate submissions
- Monitor thread no longer shuts down executor (reused by feed())

Server scheduling:
- Replace _auto_trigger_pre_decode() with _replenish_decode_buffer()
- Called on every jobs/claim (throttled to 5s intervals)
- Queries pending images, checks cache buffer level, feeds more when
  buffer drops below threshold (default 100, env IMGANALYZER_DECODE_BUFFER_SIZE)
- Images ordered by pending job count (most-needed first)

Bug fix:
- _handle_status() had pre_decode section as dead code after an early
  return statement — status now properly reports cache progress
- Progress bar shows overall cache fill (cached/total) instead of
  internal batch progress

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct 2-player card hover background color

The primary mode card hover state used #34d399 (teal/emerald), a
completely different hue from the primary #4dff00 (lime green).
On touch devices, the hover state sticks after a tap, making the
background appear to permanently change.

Changed hover background to a subtle darkened lime using color-mix,
consistent with a natural hover effect.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add internal linking library for BFS-driven wikilink insertion

Implements scripts/lib/internal-linking.ts with:
- Content index builder across 13 content directories
- BFS graph traversal from most recent reflection
- Protected region masking to avoid false matches
- Candidate discovery with longest-match-first ordering
- Gemini AI validation for link correctness
- Positional wikilink replacement engine

fix: 2-player final question skips result + add mathematical win

- Remove immediate phase:'finished' override after answer submission
  so the result/correct-answer screen is always shown before the
  end-game screen (result.ts already handles 'See Final Results' CTA)
- Add isMathematicallyWon(): ends the game early if one player's lead
  exceeds the maximum points the trailing player could ever score
  (all remaining questions + one double use)
- Add finishSession(): sets status=FINISHED and returns unanswered
  pool questions back to the pool in one place
- All three submit paths (submitAnswer, submitTop5Guess, stopTop5Early)
  and endGame now use finishSession consistently

commit.message:
fix: achievements not awarded after 2-player win

- saveMatchResult now logs errors instead of silently ignoring them
- Replace history-fetch-then-JS-filter with a direct DB count query (getMatchWinCount)
- Run checkAndAward unconditionally after every saved match instead of
  only when winner_id matches — so the win count is always accurate
  and any previously missed achievements will be caught on next save
